CP00-283 is a clean descriptor-only increment matching the mature CP272–CP282 pattern. Key confirmations:
- `production_ready: false` in both manifest and command-evidence — the gate is respected, not weakened.
- Pack binding, requirements, contract, manifest, and plan agree on risk B / 40 units / range / distributions (hand-verified the 40-unit count).
- All 7 required exports exist and contain real validation logic, not stubs.
- Authority boundary enforced: Claude read-only, not final approver; no enterprise-trust claim from local validation.
- Hermes (H08) used only as deterministic descriptor/receipt shapes, no product runtime.
- CP178+ hardening encoded in `forbidden_review_evidence`; auth preflight passed.

Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is NOT overstated: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both record production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review, so the gate is respected, not weakened. Pack binding (registry.js:199), CP283 requirements (registry.js:1121), contract projection, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and closeout-pack-plan.json all agree on risk B / 40 units / range / deliverable distribution (ui:18, implementation:11, claude_review:3, security_audit:4, fixture:2, test:1, hermes_evidence:1) and micro distribution (Primary Implementation Slice:2, Secondary Workflow Slice:22, Permission And Audit Binding:16); a hand-count of the 40 baseline units reproduces these exactly. All seven required public exports exist and are real: the descriptor/case-set builders (service.js:1623,1656) and the coverage/slice validators plus Hermes/Claude/closeout packet builders (validators.js:2510-2691), wildcard-exported through index.js, perform substantive drift, no-leak, and authority-boundary checks rather than returning stubs. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested (freezeCp283Result sets synthetic_only/no_real_data true and ~30 runtime/persistence flags false; NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION confirms). Authority boundary is correctly enforced end-to-end: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, with the Claude packet read_only and tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes (H08) patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor/receipt shapes with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, never as product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is encoded in forbidden_review_evidence (auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing source-inspection/raw-output references, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), and the readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON). Tests (model.test.js:739-786) and the validator script (validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs) assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, no-leak guards, authority boundaries, and contract deepEqual; command-evidence reports all node --check/--test, repo-wide validate commands, npm test, npm run build, and git diff --check at exit 0. The untracked c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt file and the stray docx are correctly classified as unrelated dirty files and excluded from pack scope.
